cards.text: Targeted subsidy reform aims to save ~RM8 billion a year — but no independent audit yet shows whether the targeting actually works.
cards.sub: Diesel rationalisation (June 2024) and electricity tariff restructuring contributed the bulk of savings to date. RON95 targeting begins in the second half of 2025.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: PADU (Pangkalan Data Utama) holds about 30.4 million individual profiles and is the official targeting backbone.
cards.s: Public reporting has flagged data-quality and security concerns since launch. Rafizi Ramli, the minister who built PADU, resigned over disagreements about how it would be used.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Verified savings figures so far come from electricity (~RM4B) and diesel (~RM4B). Headline 'rationalisation savings' should be read against these components.
cards.s: The Ministry of Finance has cited an ~RM8 billion annual savings target overall.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: No independent body publishes audited results on whether targeting reaches the right households.
cards.s: Subsidy effectiveness reporting is still done by the implementing ministries themselves. Independent evaluation infrastructure remains the gap.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: The story is no longer the savings number — it is whether targeting accuracy will ever be measured by anyone other than the ministries delivering it.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Like fixing a leaky pipe and announcing the savings before anyone has measured the new flow rate.
cards.sub: Headline savings mean little until delivery accuracy is independently audited.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Subsidy rationalisation needs an independent audit body publishing results per cohort, not the implementing ministries grading themselves.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

issue_headline: Subsidy Rationalisation Targets ~RM8 Billion Annual Savings — Delivery Mechanism Still Untested
